traceReason: repository-affecting work on a feature/ branch
taskSummary: Make project dashboard objectives editable in place as Markdown, then extend the same click-to-edit textarea blur-save pattern to the project description.
precedenceDecisions: GitHub Diamond governed branch naming, trace creation, validation, commit, push and PR creation.
ResearchOps Developer Control governed repository layers, generated static page parity and route-state tests.
Multi-Functional Team governed public-sector product assurance and avoiding unnecessary scope.
GOV.UK Design System governed textarea, keyboard access and focus-state handling.
Cloudflare governed the follow-up decision to make the project PATCH route succeed from D1 rather than an external API response.
Airtable Public API governed the follow-up decision to treat Airtable capture as secondary and non-blocking when rate-limited.
Cloudflare also governed the follow-up Pages proxy correction for preview-host API calls carrying Cloudflare Access headers into the passwordless/D1 Worker.
Cloudflare and Airtable Public API governed the follow-up project-list correction so D1 preview/cache rows remain the first-class read source and Airtable remains a secondary capture/upstream source.
Cloudflare governed the DaaS project follow-up because the missing record was a preview D1 seed gap rather than a browser rendering problem.
Cloudflare governed the DaaS ordering and content follow-up because the preview D1 payload needed the same project fields the dashboard and Projects page use for display.

Cloudflare governed the target-state screenshot follow-up because the remaining mismatch was incomplete preview D1 record content. The brand variant shown in the target screenshot was confirmed as environment-specific and left unchanged.

GOV.UK Design System, Cloudflare and Airtable Public API governed the description-edit follow-up because the project description became a keyboard-accessible textarea interaction that saves through D1 first and queues Airtable capture second.
filesRead: User target-state screenshots showing the expected DaaS/TCN description, stakeholders and user groups.
Current request to make the project description text editable with the same textarea blur-save behaviour as project objectives.
followUpFindings: The first implementation left the browser ordered-list marker visible beside the edit textarea. The marker is generated by the parent ol/li structure and is not part of the editable region, while the Markdown 1. is part of the textarea value.
Codex review identified that using a one-shot blur listener prevented retrying a failed PATCH from the still-open editor.

Acceptance follow-up confirmed that clearing an objective textarea should splice that objective out, save the shorter objectives payload, and re-render the list or empty state without orphaned editor or list-item HTML.

Persistence follow-up confirmed project PATCH was still Airtable-blocking; Airtable 429 was returned to the browser even though Cloudflare D1 must be the first-class save path. The updated route now requires D1 persistence before returning success and schedules Airtable capture separately.

Preview-load follow-up confirmed preview Pages proxying kept forwarding cf-access-jwt-assertion to the passwordless preview Worker. Without a ResearchOps session this triggered Cloudflare Access fallback and returned access_configuration_missing. The proxy now strips the Access JWT and email headers for preview-host API calls while preserving the ResearchOps session cookie.

Second dashboard-load follow-up confirmed /pages/project-dashboard/ was not included in the Pages static protected-route preflight. A signed-out dashboard URL could render the dashboard shell and then fail its project API calls. The route is now protected like /pages/projects/ and /pages/repository/.

D1 read follow-up confirmed the project route ignored preview D1 cache rows with source preview-seed and did not use partial D1 rows after Airtable was unavailable. The project route now treats preview-seed as a readable full D1 source, preserves it across D1-backed edits, and allows airtable-partial rows as a last-resort fallback.

DaaS follow-up confirmed Home Office Biometrics projects loaded from D1 preview seed, but the reported DaaS project id recdMo80h1QaNQCBk was absent from the preview project seed. A remote D1 read check was attempted but blocked because the local environment has no CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN.

DaaS ordering/content follow-up confirmed the DaaS seed used a placeholder DaaS project payload with no createdAt, objectives, user groups, stakeholders or lead researcher fields. Projects and dashboard rendering depend on those fields, so the row sorted below older seed rows and rendered empty content.

Target-state screenshot follow-up confirmed the latest DaaS view had the correct project name and objectives but still used the short placeholder description, one stakeholder and two user groups. The target screenshot showed the full TCN description, three PSG - ILEC stakeholders and seven user groups. The Home Office purple brand variant was environment-specific and not changed.

Description-edit follow-up confirmed the dashboard rendered the project description as plain text and the inline editing helpers existed only for objectives. The project record route also needed to accept description as an updatable framing field so blur-save could persist through D1 before non-blocking Airtable capture.

reviewThreads.author: chatgpt-codex-connector
reviewThreads.disposition: Added +1, removed the one-shot blur listener, added route-state coverage, validated retry behavior with local Playwright, replied, and resolved.

Codex review retry check: local Playwright preview forced the first PATCH to fail and confirmed the editor stayed open and the next blur saved without reopening

validationNotRun: npm test -- --ci did not run because node --test rejected the forwarded --ci flag.
Live Cloudflare Pages deployment verification was not run because this branch has not yet been merged or deployed.
residualRisks: The browser interaction was validated against a local static preview with mocked project API responses, not a deployed preview environment.
Live deployment will update through the normal PR merge and Pages deployment path.
Production Cloudflare Access fallback remains unchanged; the Access-header stripping correction is scoped to preview-host API proxying where the target is the passwordless preview Worker.
Any other direct dashboard URL for a project id that is absent from D1 will still depend on Airtable becoming available; this DaaS follow-up adds the known DaaS project id reported in testing.
